text: Diphenylalanine (FF) peptide, chemical formula C18H20N2O3·2(CH4O), molecular weight 364.40, is a self-assembling dipeptide building block (organic molecular crystal) and experimentally demonstrated piezoelectric biomaterial. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, chiral orthorhombic space group P212121 (No. 19), with unit cell a = 4.866 Å, b = 17.275 Å, c = 23.046 Å, Z = 4. The dipeptide self-assembles into nanotubes with hexagonal structure and possesses excellent mechanical properties with elastic modulus ranging from 19 to 27 GPa. Diphenylalanine peptide shows significant piezoelectric properties with d33 value of approximately 18 pm/V after poling. The crystal structure (CCDC 994055) was determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction, with the piezoelectric and nanogenerator properties reported by Jenkins et al. in Nano Energy (2018). This work demonstrated the first flexible FF peptide nanogenerator with vertical microrods arrays on flexible substrates for mechanical energy harvesting, achieving open-circuit voltage of up to −0.6 V.
experimental_method: Flexible nanogenerator testing under bending deformation, open-circuit voltage and short-circuit current measurements, mechanical energy harvesting characterization
computational_method: Finite Element Analysis (COMSOL Multiphysics), multiphysics modeling
property_ref_title: Piezoelectric diphenylalanine peptide for greatly improved flexible nanogenerators
